@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: af80
+project: diagnostics
+title: 'Integration test (rust-analyzer): diagnose([A]) reports A + broken dependent B, not clean C'
+---
+## What
+End-to-end integration test for `swissarmyhammer_diagnostics::diagnose` against a real `rust-analyzer`, gated at runtime on `which::which("rust-analyzer")` (skip+green when absent, matching `crates/swissarmyhammer-lsp/tests/session_rust_analyzer.rs`).
+
+Split out of task ^9fq036d (diagnose core API): the core `diagnose`, the `BlastRadiusDependents` resolver, and the broken-vs-clean selection logic are implemented and covered by model-free unit tests (`crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/diagnose.rs`). This task is the heavier e2e validation that needs both rust-analyzer AND a populated code-context index, so it was deferred to keep the core task focused.
+
+## Why it's non-trivial
+The blast radius (B depends on A) is read from the code-context SQLite index (`lsp_call_edges`/`lsp_symbols`). The test must therefore stand up the code-context indexing pipeline (symbol + call-edge collection via `lsp_communication::collect_and_persist_*`) against the fixture workspace so `get_blastradius("src/a.rs", max_hops=1)` returns B — this is the bulk of the setup, beyond just starting rust-analyzer.
+
+## Plan
+- Temp cargo workspace: `src/a.rs` (defines a fn), `src/b.rs` (calls A's fn — a real call edge), `src/c.rs` (independent, clean).
+- Start `LspDaemon` → `session`; index the workspace into a code-context DB so call edges A←B exist.
+- Edit A to break it (change/remove the fn B calls) and sync; `diagnose(session, ["src/a.rs"], config, BlastRadiusDependents::new(&conn), TokioTimer)`.
+- Assert: report contains A's error AND B as a broken dependent; does NOT contain clean file C.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Runtime-gated; green-skips without rust-analyzer.
+- [ ] Asserts A error + B-as-broken folded in, C excluded.
+
+#diagnostics
\ No newline at end of file

+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed. New e2e test: crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/tests/diagnose_rust_analyzer.rs (gated on which::which("rust-analyzer"), SERIAL tokio::Mutex, bounded waits + 30s timeout on diagnose, daemon kill_on_drop). Fixture: compilable cargo lib crate (lib.rs declares mods a/b/c); src/a.rs defines alpha()->i32, src/b.rs `use crate::a::alpha; let x: i32 = alpha()` (real call edge A<-B), src/c.rs independent/clean.
+
+    Index setup: tree-sitter call-edge pipeline (insert ts_chunks -> ensure_ts_symbols -> generate_ts_call_edges -> write_ts_edges) populates lsp_call_edges/lsp_symbols so get_blastradius("src/a.rs",1) returns src/b.rs. This is the same pipeline that backs get_blastradius (collect_and_persist_* LSP-edge collection is the alternative; the tree-sitter heuristic is deterministic and is what test_get_blastradius uses, so chosen for robustness). Test asserts the edge exists in the DB before going near the server.
+
+    KEY DISCOVERY (cost ~3 min each on two dead ends): (1) the daemon read loop does NOT pump push-model publishDiagnostics into the session cache — only the pull (refresh_file -> textDocument/diagnostic) makes a change observable (documented in watcher.rs refresh_file). diagnose() itself only sync_open+settle, so the cache must be pre-warmed via pull. Test drives refresh_file in a bounded poll. (2) rust-analyzer's pull does NOT reliably surface unresolved-name/unresolved-import diagnostics quickly without flycheck; native type-inference mismatches DO. So the break is: change alpha's return i32->&'static str + a local type error in A; B's `let x: i32 = alpha()` becomes a native type mismatch. Both A and B errors then surface in ~3s. Runtime: 3.44s with rust-analyzer present.
+
+    Path-space bridging: test relativizes the abs target for the blast-radius lookup and absolutizes returned dependents back, exactly as diagnostics/mod.rs produce_outcome does, then feeds PrecomputedDependents to diagnose. Workspace root canonicalized (macOS /var -> /private/var) so watched uris match the server's canonicalized uris.
+
+    PRODUCTION FIX (symlink concern was REAL, not already-correct): the tool's repo_root() did NOT canonicalize — find_git_repository_root_from returns the raw (possibly symlinked) path, so repo.join(rel) absolutes would be /var/... while rust-analyzer publishes under /private/var/..., silently dropping a target's own diagnostics. Fixed crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/diagnostics/mod.rs repo_root() to std::fs::canonicalize the resolved root (fallback to non-canonical on failure). Added repo_root_canonicalizes_a_symlinked_working_dir unit test; confirmed red (returns /var/.../repo-link) without the fix, green with it.
